title: Core Python — Variables, Strings, Numbers & Print
intro: Before you write tools that scan networks, you must first write simple programs that print results clearly. Master variables, strings, and numbers. The cybersecurity tools you will build this term are made of these building blocks.

exercises.prompt: Print a two-line banner with a centered title. Line 1: exactly "========================". Line 2: exactly "   Recon Report". Hints: You will write two print statements.

checks.label: prints the exact banner with correct spacing

hints: Type the text exactly as shown — spaces matter.

exercises.prompt: Set a variable `name` to "Maya". Using an f-string, print the sentence "Hello, Maya."

checks.label: prints the greeting with the correct name

checks.name: name
checks.label: name variable is set to Maya

hints: An f-string starts with an `f` before the quote: f"Hello, {name}."

exercises.prompt: Set a variable `ports` to the result of adding 20 and 2. Print the sentence "22 ports" using an f-string.

checks.label: prints 22 ports with the calculated value

hints: Then print with an f-string: f"{ports} ports"

exercises.prompt: You are given two variables: `first="Ada"` and `last="Lovelace"`. Using string concatenation or an f-string, print "Lovelace, Ada" (last name first).
exercises.starter:
first = "Ada"
last = "Lovelace"
# Print last name, first name

checks.label: prints last name followed by first name

hints: You can join strings: last + ", " + first

exercises.prompt: Set a variable `count` to `int("443")` (convert the string "443" to an integer). Print the result of `count + 1` using an f-string or concatenation.
hints: Use int() to convert text to a number: count = int("443")

stretch.prompt: Stretch: You work with profiles. Set three variables: `name="Jordan"`, `role="analyst"`, and `id="SEC-001"`. Using an f-string, print "Jordan | analyst | SEC-001" with pipes and spaces.
stretch.starter: # Build and print the profile line

checks.label: prints the profile line with correct pipes and spacing

hints: Build it in one line with an f-string: f"{name} | {role} | {id}"
